# Fire PDF Pro
> Fire PDF Pro is a B2B SaaS tool that automates FDNY paperwork 
> and NYC property intelligence workflows for fire alarm contractors, 
> expeditors, and filing professionals operating in New York City.

Fire PDF Pro eliminates manual data entry for FDNY form submissions 
by pulling live building data from the NYC Department of Buildings (DOB) 
and pre-filling the required forms instantly. It reduces filing errors, 
rejected submissions, and administrative overhead for NYC fire alarm 
professionals.

## Core Features
- Auto-fill FDNY Form TM-1 (Technical Manual)
- Auto-fill FDNY Form A-433 (Device Schedule)
- Auto-fill FDNY Form B-45 (Contractor Certification)
- Instant NYC BIN (Building Identification Number) lookup
- NYC DOB property intelligence (occupancy class, construction type, stories, year built)
- Vendor and architect profile saving
- Project history and re-generation
- Fast PDF generation and download

## Problems It Solves
- Eliminates manual typing of building data into FDNY forms
- Reduces DOB research time per job
- Prevents common filing mistakes and rejected submissions
- Replaces slow, manual PDF workflows
- Saves hours of administrative work per week

## Target Users
- NYC fire alarm contractors
- FDNY expeditors
- Fire alarm filing representatives
- Fire protection engineers
- Low voltage contractors working in NYC
- Architects and DOB consultants filing fire alarm permits

## FDNY Forms Covered
- TM-1: Technical Manual Form 1, required for fire alarm system installations in NYC
- A-433: Device schedule form listing fire alarm devices per floor
- B-45: Contractor certification form submitted with fire alarm filings

## NYC Coverage
- Manhattan
- Brooklyn
- Queens
- The Bronx
- Staten Island

## Key Pages
- Homepage: https://firepdfpro.com
- Pricing: https://firepdfpro.com/pricing

- Bronx FDNY Forms: https://firepdfpro.com/bronx-fdny-forms
- Staten Island FDNY Forms: https://firepdfpro.com/staten-island-fdny-forms

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is the TM-1 form?
A: The TM-1 is an FDNY Technical Manual form required for fire alarm 
system installations in New York City. It includes building information, 
system type, occupancy classification, and contractor details.

Q: What is the A-433 form?
A: The A-433 is the FDNY device schedule form that lists all fire alarm 
devices installed per floor in a building. It must accompany the TM-1 
in a complete fire alarm filing.

Q: What is the B-45 form?
A: The B-45 is the FDNY contractor certification form. It certifies that 
the fire alarm system was installed by a licensed contractor in compliance 
with NYC fire code.

Q: What is a BIN number in NYC?
A: A BIN (Building Identification Number) is a unique 7-digit identifier 
assigned by the NYC Department of Buildings to every building in New York 
City. It is required on FDNY fire alarm filing forms.

Q: How does Fire PDF Pro get building data?
A: Fire PDF Pro queries the NYC Geoclient API and NYC DOB open data in 
real time using the address or BIN entered by the user. It returns 
occupancy group, construction class, stories, year built, and other 
data required for FDNY forms.

Q: Which NYC boroughs does Fire PDF Pro support?
A: Fire PDF Pro supports all five NYC boroughs: Manhattan, Brooklyn, 
Queens, The Bronx, and Staten Island.

Q: Who uses Fire PDF Pro?
A: Fire PDF Pro is used by NYC fire alarm contractors, FDNY expeditors, 
filing representatives, fire protection engineers, and low voltage 
contractors who regularly submit fire alarm filings to the FDNY.

## Company
- Product: Fire PDF Pro
- Operator: GLA Enterprise
- Website: https://firepdfpro.com
- Industry: NYC fire alarm compliance, FDNY paperwork automation
- Market: New York City B2B compliance software

## Data Sources
Fire PDF Pro uses publicly available data from:
- NYC Department of Buildings (DOB) Open Data
- NYC Geoclient API
- FDNY official form templates

## Legal
Fire PDF Pro is an independent private software tool. It is not 
affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the NYC Fire Department 
(FDNY), the NYC Department of Buildings (DOB), or any government agency. 
Generated forms are drafting aids and must be reviewed before official 
submission.
